title: One-Lap DP Policy-Value Features
summary: Recent F1 OR papers model pit timing as a sequential decision where stop-now, stop-later, and rival interaction change optimal behavior; the current feature stack approximates tyre windows but not the explicit value gap between actions.
rationale: Recent F1 OR papers model pit timing as a sequential decision where stop-now, stop-later, and rival interaction change optimal behavior; the current feature stack approximates tyre windows but not the explicit value gap between actions.
implementation_hint: Build a lightweight offline policy module that scores `pit_now`, `pit_next_lap`, and `go_long` using estimated tyre life, laps remaining, pit-loss prior, and local rival proximity. Feed only the value differences or action argmax indicators into the existing tabular classifier.
expected_effect: Should improve ordering of borderline rows where tyre-life heuristics and track-position incentives disagree.
risk: If the rival or pit-loss proxy is too crude, the policy feature can become a noisy hand-crafted heuristic and overfit CV.
